description.p: redx scans a folder recursively, classifies every subdirectory as empty or not, and lets you delete the empty ones to trash (or permanently) from a colour-coded tree view.

Custom rules control what counts as empty. Add glob patterns such as *.tmp or Thumbs.db to the ignore list, and any directory whose only files match those patterns is treated as empty. Zero-byte files have their own toggle. Specific branches can be marked Protected from a right-click menu so they survive a bulk delete.
A Linux port of the Windows utility RED (Remove-Empty-Directories). Written in Python with PySide6 (Qt 6). Three delete modes: simulate (no changes), move to trash (reversible), or permanent.

screenshot.caption: Scan results: empty directories in red, mixed parents in gray
Filters tab: glob-style ignore patterns for files and directories

ul.li: Recursive scanner with symlink shield and glob-pattern ignore lists
Tree view with per-directory empty / not-empty / protected colouring
Three delete modes: move to trash, permanent, simulate (dry run)
Right-click Protect on tree nodes; protection propagates to ancestors
Drag-and-drop folder onto the window to set scan target
Settings persist across sessions and survive ungraceful exits
Refuses to scan system mountpoints (/proc, /sys, /dev, etc.)
